briefs.title: Creatine monohydrate at 3g/day — does the loading phase actually matter?
briefs.key: A re-analysis of muscle PCr saturation studies suggests 28 days at 3g matches a 5-day loading protocol within ±2% on every measured endpoint. Loading is a marketing artifact.
briefs.author: Adrian Kojima, PhD

briefs.title: Magnesium L-threonate — the cognition claim re-examined
briefs.key: Manufacturer-funded rat studies vs the two human trials. The signal is small and the noise is large.
briefs.author: Dr. Park

briefs.title: Heavy metal contamination in greens powders — round 4 data
briefs.key: 17 of 24 greens products tested above CA Prop. 65 cadmium thresholds. Detailed ppm tables.
briefs.author: Adrian K. + lab team

briefs.title: Vitamin D3 vs. D2 — bioequivalence at typical supplement doses
briefs.key: D3 raises 25(OH)D approximately 1.4× more efficiently than D2 at oral doses ≤2,000 IU/day.
briefs.author: Dr. Park

briefs.title: Ashwagandha (KSM-66 vs Sensoril) — the cortisol claim, examined
briefs.key: Both extracts modestly reduce salivary cortisol in stressed subjects at ≥600mg/day for 8+ weeks. Effect sizes smaller than the marketing suggests.
briefs.author: Adrian K.

briefs.title: BCAAs in resistance training — do they do anything if you eat enough protein?
briefs.key: No. The signal disappears once total daily protein crosses 1.6 g/kg.
briefs.author: Adrian K.

briefs.title: The melatonin labeling problem — independent assay of 17 retail brands
briefs.key: Label-stated dose vs. assayed dose. Median deviation: +71%, with one brand at +478%. Implications for pediatric use are non-trivial.
briefs.author: Dr. Park + lab team
